sections.title: Starting Demon Slayer

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: Hello young one. Cross my palm with silver and the future will be revealed to you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh dear. I don't have any money.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, here you go.

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: Come closer, and listen carefully to what the future holds for you, as I peer into the swirling mists of the crystal ball.

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: I can see images forming. I can see you.

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: You are holding a very impressive looking sword. I'm sure I recognise that sword...

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: There is a big dark shadow appearing now.

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: Aaargh!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you all right?

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: It's Delrith! Delrith is coming!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who's Delrith?

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: Delrith...

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: Delrith is a powerful demon.

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: Oh! I really hope he didn't see me looking at him through my crystal ball!

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: He tried to destroy this city 150 years ago. He was stopped just in time by the great hero Wally.

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: Using his magic sword Silverlight, Wally managed to trap the demon in the stone circle just south of this city.

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: Ye gods! Silverlight was the sword you were holding in my vision! You are the one destined to stop the demon this time.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How am I meant to fight a demon who can destroy cities?!

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: If you face Delrith while he is still weak from being summoned, and use the correct weapon, you will not find the task too arduous.

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: Do not fear. If you follow the path of the great hero Wally, then you are sure to defeat the demon.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, where is he? I'll kill him for you!

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: Ah, the overconfidence of the young!

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: Delrith can't be harmed by ordinary weapons. You must face him using the same weapon that Wally used.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wally doesn't sound a very heroic name.

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: Yes I know. Maybe that is why history doesn't remember him. However he was a very great hero.

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: Who knows how much pain and suffering Delrith would have brought forth without Wally to stop him!

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: It looks like you are going to need to perform similar heroics.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So how did Wally kill Delrith?

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: Wally managed to arrive at the stone circle just as Delrith was summoned by a cult of chaos druids...

lines.speaker: Wally
lines.text: Die, foul demon!

lines.speaker: Wally
lines.text: Now, what was that incantation again?

lines.speaker: Wally
lines.text: [word 1]... [word 2]... [word 3]... [word 4]... [word 5]!

lines.speaker: Wally
lines.text: I am the greatest demon slayer EVER!

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: By reciting the correct magical incantation, and thrusting Silverlight into Delrith while he was newly summoned, Wally was able to imprison Delrith in the stone block in the centre of the circle.

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: Delrith will come forth from the stone circle again.

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: I would imagine an evil sorcerer is already starting on the rituals to summon Delrith as we speak.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What is the magical incantation?

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: Oh yes, let me think a second...

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: Alright, I think I've got it now, it goes.... [word 1]... [word 2]... [word 3]... [word 4]... [word 5]. Have you got that?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think so, yes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I find Silverlight?

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: Silverlight has been passed down through Wally's descendants. I believe it is currently in the care of one of the King's knights called Sir Prysin.

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: He shouldn't be too hard to find. He lives in the royal palace in this city. Tell him Aris sent you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok thanks. I'll do my best to stop the demon.

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: Good luck, and may Guthix be with you!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I don't believe in that stuff.

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: Ok suit yourself.

sections.title: Aris after starting the quest

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: Greetings. How goes thy quest?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm still working on it.

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: Well if you need any advice I'm always here, young one.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Stop calling me that!

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: In the scheme of things you are very young.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, but how old are you?

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: Count the number of legs on the stools in the Blue Moon inn, and multiply that number by seven.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er, yeah, whatever.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh if it's in the scheme of things that's ok.

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: You show wisdom for one so young.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well I'd better press on with it.

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: See you anon.

sections.title: Sir Prysin dialogue
Talking to Sir Prysin again before getting keys

lines.speaker: Sir Prysin
lines.text: So how are you doing with getting the keys?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I haven't found any of them yet.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you remind me where all the keys were again?

lines.speaker: Sir Prysin
lines.text: I kept one of the keys. I gave the other two to other people for safe keeping.

lines.speaker: Sir Prysin
lines.text: One I gave to Rovin, the captain of the palace guard.

lines.speaker: Sir Prysin
lines.text: I gave the other to the wizard Traiborn.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm still looking.

lines.speaker: Sir Prysin
lines.text: Ok, tell me when you've got them all.

sections.title: Returning to Sir Prysin with one key

lines.speaker: Sir Prysin
lines.text: So how are you doing with getting the keys?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've got the key from Captain Rovin.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've got the key from Wizard Traiborn.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've got the key which you dropped down the drain.

sections.title: Talking to Sir Prysin with two keys

lines.speaker: Sir Prysin
lines.text: So how are you doing with getting the keys?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've got the keys from Wizard Traiborn and Captain Rovin.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've got the key from Captain Rovin and the one that you dropped down the drain.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've got the key from Wizard Traiborn and the one that you dropped down the drain.

lines.speaker: Sir Prysin
lines.text: Hello, who are you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am a mighty adventurer, who are you?

lines.speaker: Sir Prysin
lines.text: I am Sir Prysin. A bold and famous knight of the realm.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I was hoping you could tell me.

lines.speaker: Sir Prysin
lines.text: Well I've never met you before.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Aris said I should come and talk to you.

lines.speaker: Sir Prysin
lines.text: Aris? Is she still alive? I remember her from when I was pretty young. Well what do you need to talk to me about?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need to find Silverlight.

lines.speaker: Sir Prysin
lines.text: What do you need to find that for?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need it to fight Delrith.

lines.speaker: Sir Prysin
lines.text: Delrith? I thought the world was rid of him, thanks to my great-grandfather.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well Aris' crystal ball seems to think otherwise.

lines.speaker: Sir Prysin
lines.text: Well if the ball says so, I'd better help you.

lines.speaker: Sir Prysin
lines.text: The problem is getting Silverlight.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You mean you don't have it?

lines.speaker: Sir Prysin
lines.text: Oh I do have it, but it is so powerful that the king made me put it in a special box which needs three different keys to open it. That way it won't fall into the wrong hands.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So give me the keys!

lines.speaker: Sir Prysin
lines.text: Um, well, it's not so easy.

lines.speaker: Sir Prysin
lines.text: I kept one of the keys. I gave the other two to other people for safe keeping.

lines.speaker: Sir Prysin
lines.text: One I gave to Rovin, the captain of the palace guard.

lines.speaker: Sir Prysin
lines.text: I gave the other to the wizard Traiborn.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you give me your key?

lines.speaker: Sir Prysin
lines.text: Well there's a problem there as well.

lines.speaker: Sir Prysin
lines.text: I managed to drop the key in the drain just outside the palace kitchen. It is just inside and I can't reach it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what does the drain connect to?

lines.speaker: Sir Prysin
lines.text: It is the drain for the drainpipe running from the sink in the kitchen down to the palace sewers.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well I'd better go key hunting.

lines.speaker: Sir Prysin
lines.text: Ok, goodbye.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I find Captain Rovin?

lines.speaker: Sir Prysin
lines.text: Captain Rovin lives at the top of the guards' quarters in the north-west wing of this palace.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where does the wizard live?

lines.speaker: Sir Prysin
lines.text: Wizard Traiborn?

lines.speaker: Sir Prysin
lines.text: He is one of the wizards who lives in the tower on the little island just off the south coast. I believe his quarters are on the first floor of the tower.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And why is this a problem?

lines.speaker: Sir Prysin
lines.text: I kept one of the keys. I gave the other two to other people for safe keeping.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He's back and unfortunately I've got to deal with him.

lines.speaker: Sir Prysin
lines.text: You don't look up to much. I suppose Silverlight may be good enough to carry you through though.

lines.speaker: Sir Prysin
lines.text: The problem is getting Silverlight.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You mean you don't have it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes she is still alive. She lives right outside the castle!

lines.speaker: Sir Prysin
lines.text: Oh, is that the same Aris? I would have thought she would have died by now. She was pretty old when I was a lad.

lines.speaker: Sir Prysin
lines.text: Anyway, what can I do for you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need to find Silverlight.

sections.title: Getting the key from Captain Rovin
Talking to Captain Rovin again after obtaining the key

lines.speaker: Captain Rovin
lines.text: What are you doing up here? Only the palace guards are allowed up here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I know, but this is important.

lines.speaker: Captain Rovin
lines.text: Ok, I'm listening. Tell me what's so important.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There's a demon who wants to invade the city.

lines.speaker: Captain Rovin
lines.text: Yes, you said before, haven't you killed it yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well I'm going to use the powerful sword Silverlight, which I believe you have one of the keys for?

lines.speaker: Captain Rovin
lines.text: I already gave you my key. Check your pockets.

lines.speaker: Captain Rovin
lines.text: I already gave you my key. Maybe you left it somewhere. Have you checked your bank account?

lines.speaker: Captain Rovin
lines.text: What are you doing up here? Only the palace guards are allowed up here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I know, but this is important.

lines.speaker: Captain Rovin
lines.text: Ok, I'm listening. Tell me what's so important.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There's a demon who wants to invade the city.

lines.speaker: Captain Rovin
lines.text: Is it a powerful demon?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not really.

lines.speaker: Captain Rovin
lines.text: Well, I'm sure the palace guards can deal with it, then. Thanks for the information.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, very.

lines.speaker: Captain Rovin
lines.text: As good as the palace guards are, I don't know if they're up to taking on a very powerful demon.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeah, the palace guards are rubbish!

lines.speaker: Captain Rovin
lines.text: Yeah, they're--

lines.speaker: Captain Rovin
lines.text: Wait! How dare you insult the palace guards? Get out of my sight!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's not them who are going to fight the demon, it's me.

lines.speaker: Captain Rovin
lines.text: What, all by yourself? How are you going to do that?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm going to use the powerful sword Silverlight, which I believe you have one of the keys for?

lines.speaker: Captain Rovin
lines.text: Yes, I do. But why should I give it to you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fortune-teller Aris said I was destined to kill the demon.

lines.speaker: Captain Rovin
lines.text: A fortune-teller? Destiny? I don't believe in that stuff. I got where I am today by hard work, not by destiny! Why should I care what that mad old fortune-teller says?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Otherwise the demon will destroy the city!

lines.speaker: Captain Rovin
lines.text: You can't fool me! How do I know you haven't just made that story up to get my key?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sir Prysin said you would give me the key.

lines.speaker: Captain Rovin
lines.text: Oh, he did, did he? Well I don't report to Sir Prysin, I report directly to the king!

lines.speaker: Captain Rovin
lines.text: I didn't work my way up through the ranks of the palace guards so I could take orders from an ill-bred moron who only has his job because his great- grandfather was a hero with a silly name!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why did he give you one of the keys then?

lines.speaker: Captain Rovin
lines.text: Only because the king ordered him to! The king couldn't get Sir Prysin to part with his precious ancestral sword, but he made him lock it up so he couldn't lose it.

lines.speaker: Captain Rovin
lines.text: I got one key and I think some wizard got another. Now what happened to the third one?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sir Prysin dropped it down a drain!

lines.speaker: Captain Rovin
lines.text: Ha ha ha! The idiot!

lines.speaker: Captain Rovin
lines.text: Okay, I'll give you the key, just so that it's you that kills the demon and not Sir Prysin!

sections.title: Searching the drain near the palace kitchen
Pouring water in the drain

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: OK, I think I've washed the key down into the sewer. I'd better go down and get it!

sections.title: Pouring more water down the drain
Searching the drain after washing down the key/outside the quest
Picking up the key from the sewers

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That must be the key Sir Prysin dropped.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't seem to be able to reach it. I wonder if I can dislodge it somehow. That way it may go down into the sewers.

sections.title: Getting the key from Traiborn
Returning with no bones to Traiborn

lines.speaker: Wizard Traiborn
lines.text: Ello young thingummywut.

lines.speaker: Wizard Traiborn
lines.text: How are you doing finding bones?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I haven't got any at the moment.

lines.speaker: Wizard Traiborn
lines.text: Nevermind, keep working on it.

sections.title: Returning with some bones to Traiborn

lines.speaker: Wizard Traiborn
lines.text: Ello young thingummywut.

lines.speaker: Wizard Traiborn
lines.text: How are you doing finding bones?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have some bones.

lines.speaker: Wizard Traiborn
lines.text: Give 'em here then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's all of them.

lines.speaker: Wizard Traiborn
lines.text: I still need [25-X] more.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, I'll keep looking.

sections.title: Returning with the last of the bones to Traiborn

lines.speaker: Wizard Traiborn
lines.text: Ello young thingummywut.

lines.speaker: Wizard Traiborn
lines.text: How are you doing finding bones?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have some bones.

lines.speaker: Wizard Traiborn
lines.text: Give 'em here then.

lines.speaker: Wizard Traiborn
lines.text: Hurrah! That's all 25 sets of bones.

lines.speaker: Wizard Traiborn
lines.text: Wings of dark and colour too, Spreading in the morning dew; Locked away I have a key; Return it now, please, unto me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thank you very much.

lines.speaker: Wizard Traiborn
lines.text: Not a problem for a friend of Sir What's-his-face.

sections.title: Traiborn after obtaining the key

lines.speaker: Wizard Traiborn
lines.text: Ello young thingummywut.

lines.speaker: Wizard Traiborn
lines.text: Don't you have somewhere to be, young thingummywut? You still have that key you asked me for.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You're right. I've got a demon to slay.

sections.title: Traiborn after losing the key

lines.speaker: Wizard Traiborn
lines.text: Ello young thingummywut.

lines.speaker: Wizard Traiborn
lines.text: Ello young thingummywut.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've lost the key you gave to me.

lines.speaker: Wizard Traiborn
lines.text: Yes I know, it was returned to me. If you want it back you're going to have to collect another 25 sets of bones.

sections.title: Returning to Sir Prysin with all the keys

lines.speaker: Sir Prysin
lines.text: So how are you doing with getting the keys?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've got all three keys!

lines.speaker: Sir Prysin
lines.text: Excellent! Now I can give you Silverlight.

lines.speaker: Sir Prysin
lines.text: That sword belonged to my great-grandfather. Make sure you treat it with respect!

lines.speaker: Sir Prysin
lines.text: Now go kill that demon!

sections.title: After talking to Sir Prysin

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: How goes the quest?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I found Sir Prysin. Unfortunately I haven't got the sword yet. He's made it complicated for me.

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: Ok, hurry, we haven't much time.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well I'd better press on with it.

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: See you anon.

sections.title: Talking to Sir Prysin before fighting Delrith

lines.speaker: Sir Prysin
lines.text: Have you sorted that demon out yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, not yet.

lines.speaker: Sir Prysin
lines.text: Well get on with it. He'll be pretty powerful when he gets to full strength.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet. And I, um, lost Silverlight.

lines.speaker: Sir Prysin
lines.text: Yes, I know, someone returned it to me. Take better care of it this time.

sections.title: Aris after acquiring the sword

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: How goes the quest?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have the sword now. I just need to kill the demon, I think.

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: Yep, that's right.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I find the demon?

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: Just head south and you should find the stone circle just outside the city gate.

lines.speaker: Wizard Traiborn
lines.text: Ello young thingummywut.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need to get a key given to you by Sir Prysin.

lines.speaker: Wizard Traiborn
lines.text: Sir Prysin? Who's that? What would I want his key for?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He told me you were looking after it for him.

lines.speaker: Wizard Traiborn
lines.text: That wasn't very clever of him. I'd lose my head if it wasn't screwed on. Go and tell him to find someone else to look after his valuables in future.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I'll go and tell him that.

lines.speaker: Wizard Traiborn
lines.text: Oh that's great, if it wouldn't be too much trouble.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Err I'd better be off really.

lines.speaker: Wizard Traiborn
lines.text: Oh ok, have a good time, and watch out for sheep! They're more cunning than they look.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He's one of the King's knights.

lines.speaker: Wizard Traiborn
lines.text: Say, I remember one of the King's knights. He had nice shoes...

lines.speaker: Wizard Traiborn
lines.text: ...and didn't like my homemade spinach rolls. Would you like a spinach roll?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes please.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thank you very much.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Just tell me if you have the key.

lines.speaker: Wizard Traiborn
lines.text: The key? The key to what?

lines.speaker: Wizard Traiborn
lines.text: There's more than one key in the world don't you know? Would be a bit odd if there was only the one.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's the key to get a sword called Silverlight.

lines.speaker: Wizard Traiborn
lines.text: Silverlight? Never heard of that. Sounds a good name for a ship. Are you sure it's not the name of a ship rather than a sword?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeah, pretty sure.

lines.speaker: Wizard Traiborn
lines.text: That's a pity, waste of a name.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You've lost it haven't you?

lines.speaker: Wizard Traiborn
lines.text: Me? Lose things? That's a nasty accusation.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, have you got any keys knocking around?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, have you got any keys knocking around?

lines.speaker: Wizard Traiborn
lines.text: Now you come to mention it, yes I do have a key. It's in my special closet of valuable stuff. Now how do I get into that?

lines.speaker: Wizard Traiborn
lines.text: I sealed it using one of my magic rituals. So it would make sense that another ritual would open it again.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So do you know what ritual to use?

lines.speaker: Wizard Traiborn
lines.text: Let me think a second.

lines.speaker: Wizard Traiborn
lines.text: Yes a simple drazier style ritual should suffice. Hmm, main problem with that is I'll need 25 sets of bones. Now where am I going to get hold of something like that?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm, that's too bad. I really need that key.

lines.speaker: Wizard Traiborn
lines.text: Ah well, sorry I couldn't be any more help.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll help get the bones for you.

lines.speaker: Wizard Traiborn
lines.text: Ooh that would be very good of you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I'll speak to you when I've got some bones.

sections.title: The final battle
Attempting to attack Delrith without Silverlight
After lowering Delrith's Hitpoints to 0

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Now what was that incantation again?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: [word 1]...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: [word 2]...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: [word 3]...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: [word 4]...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: [word 5]!

lines.speaker: Denath
lines.text: Arise, O mighty Delrith! Bring destruction to this soft, weak city!

lines.speaker: Dark wizards
lines.text: Arise, Delrith!

lines.speaker: Denath
lines.text: Ha ha ha! At last you are free, my demonic brother! Rest now, and then have your revenge on this pitiful city!

lines.speaker: Dark wizard
lines.text: Who's that?

lines.speaker: Denath
lines.text: Noo! Not Silverlight! Delrith is not ready yet!

lines.speaker: Denath
lines.text: I've got to get out of here...
